title: aglaia sapindina
intro: Aglaia sapindina, also known as the yellow aglaia or kembang rayap, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Meliaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Aglaia sapindina include its potential to treat various diseases such as malaria, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Herbal preparations of Aglaia sapindina are often made from the leaves, bark, and roots, and can be consumed as infusions, decoctions, or extracts to harness its medicinal properties.
